commands.review_summary:
The PR tightens `downloadUrl()` so unsafe decimal `Content-Length` values are rejected before body reads and adds regression coverage for cancellation and temp-file cleanup.

PR surface: Tests +44, Other +3. Total +47 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes from source and the PR body's direct `downloadUrl()` repro: current main turns an unsafe decimal `Content-Length` into an unsafe Number, fails the safe-integer guard, and falls through to body reading.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed because the patch is focused and I found no actionable review finding; remaining action is maintainer draft handling and validation.

target.title: fix(scripts): reject unsafe package download lengths
target.body:
## Summary

- Reject unsafe decimal `Content-Length` values for `package_url` downloads before streaming the response body.
- Add regression coverage that proves the unsafe declared length is rejected before the body reader starts and still cancels/cleans temporary output.

## Verification

- `node --check scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs`
- Direct `downloadUrl()` repro with `content-length: 9007199254740993`, `maxBytes: 64`, and a custom never-completing body reader now fails in ~25ms with `package_url exceeds maximum download size of 64 bytes`; `readStarted=false`, `bodyCancelled=true`.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` clean, overall patch correct `0.88`.

## Notes

Focused Vitest was not run locally because this sparse GWT has no `node_modules`, and this repo lane must not install dependencies in Codex worktrees. I will use the hosted exact-head release gate for broad proof.
target.author: vincentkoc
